questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Which of the following is a common qualification required for both the Vice-President of India and a member of the Rajya Sabha?

options.text: Must have completed 30 years of age.
Must be a citizen of India.
Must be a member of either House of Parliament.
Must not hold any office of profit under a local authority.

questions.explanation: While both must be citizens of India, their age requirements differ (35 for VP, 30 for RS). The VP must be 'qualified for election' as a member of the Rajya Sabha but cannot be a sitting member of either House. Both must not hold an office of profit, but the citizenship requirement is the most fundamental shared qualification explicitly listed.
questions.takeaway: The Vice-President must meet Rajya Sabha eligibility criteria but has a higher age threshold (35 years).
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Qualifications [for Vice-President]: Citizen of India. 35 years Age. He is qualified for election as a member of the Council of States (Rajya Sabha). Must not hold any office of profit.
